title: Concurrency, Shared State, Backpressure, Rate Limits, and Load Shedding · Program Defense
summary: A production-readiness review compares near-miss decisions for concurrency, shared state, backpressure, rate limits, and load shedding and requires the smallest safe concurrency budget and overload decision model with changed-request evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for concurrency budget and overload decision model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Treat handlers as concurrent, protect shared state with ownership or synchronization, and verify with race tests under changed schedules.
Bound active expensive work with semaphores, worker pools, queue capacity, context-aware admission, fairness, and per-tenant partitions.
Choose trusted identity, token bucket or window semantics, burst, refill, distributed consistency, Retry-After, and bypass policy from abuse risk.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
